$comment: Example Agent — Minimal starter configuration for a2a-wrapper-opencode.
Use this as a copy-paste starting point when building your own agent:
cp -r agents/example agents/my-agent
# Edit agents/my-agent/config.json for your use case
./agents/my-agent/start.sh start
This agent has no external MCP tool servers. To connect one, add an 'mcp'
section — see the commented-out block at the bottom of this file for the
shape of both local (stdio) and remote (HTTP/SSE) MCP server configs.
The agent card is served at GET /agent/card and advertises this agent to
A2A-compatible orchestrators and callers. Fill in a clear name and
description — they are the first thing callers see.
agentCard.name: Example Agent
agentCard.description: A minimal example agent. Replace this description with what your agent actually does.

skills.$comment: Skills are optional but help orchestrators understand agent capabilities without calling it experimentally.
skills.name: General Assistant
skills.description: Answer questions, reason through problems, and assist with general tasks

$comment: Each agent must use a unique port when running multiple agents on the same host.
Change advertiseHost to your machine's IP or domain when running behind Docker or a proxy.
Set advertiseProtocol to 'https' when running behind a TLS-terminating proxy.
Controls how the wrapper connects to OpenCode.
baseUrl         — URL of the running OpenCode server process.
model           — The LLM provider/model string OpenCode accepts.
Examples: 'github-copilot/gpt-4o', 'anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet',
'openai/gpt-4o'
systemPromptMode:
'replace'     — A production-ready preamble is prepended to your prompt,
preventing tool/internals disclosure. Recommended for production.
'append'      — Your prompt is injected as-is before the first user message.
opencode.systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Replace this system prompt with instructions that define your agent's role, persona, and expected behaviour.

$comment: autoApprovePermissions — Automatically approve all OpenCode permission dialogs.
autoAnswerQuestions    — Automatically answer OpenCode question prompts.
streamArtifactChunks  — false (default): buffer and send a single artifact.
true: stream artifact chunks incrementally (spec-correct).
enablePollingFallback  — Fall back to HTTP polling when SSE streaming fails.
prompt          — Maximum milliseconds to wait for a full response.
Increase for agents that call slow external MCP tools.
pollingInterval — How often (ms) to poll for response updates when using
the polling fallback.
healthCheck     — How often (ms) to check OpenCode server health. 0 to disable.

logging.$comment: level — One of: 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'. Use 'debug' during development.
